weather.summary: Saturday: 10°C, light rain. Sunday: 11°C, light rain

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with exciting indoor activities perfect for a 3-year-old! I've found a great mix of free museum adventures, interactive exhibits, and engaging theatre performances to keep your little one entertained, especially with the rainy weather forecast.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed space with books and stories suitable for a wide range of ages, offering a quiet and engaging activity.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hackney Downs
transport.tip: Check term times before visiting

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Museum of childhood with interactive exhibits and sensory play areas.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for children with sensory experiences, hands-on exhibits, and space to move around, making it ideal for a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Central line to Bethnal Green, then a short walk
transport.tip: Check for specific events and workshops on their website.

activities.title: Inside Aardman: Wallace & Gromit and Friends
activities.description: Exhibition exploring how iconic Aardman characters are brought to life with books, costumes, puppets, and artwork.
activities.whyItWorks: Even though the suggested age is older, the visual and tactile nature of the exhibition could still engage a 3-year-old, especially if they are already familiar with the characters.

transport.fromHackney: Central line to Bethnal Green, then a short walk
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as it's a popular exhibition.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small, beautifully designed wooden playground with a pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for toddlers, this playground offers a safe and engaging space for play with a fun theme.

transport.tip: Combine with a walk around the Olympic Park.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Museum showcasing the history of London's Docklands with child-friendly exhibits and activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Features hands-on activities and activity sheets designed for different ages, with special red signs explaining things in a child-friendly way.

transport.tip: Visit the Mudlarks soft play area.

activities.title: Sound Explorers at Young V&A
activities.description: Interactive sessions exploring sound and music.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on and sensory, allowing for creative exploration of sounds.

transport.tip: Check the Young V&A website for the schedule and any booking requirements.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed space with books and stories suitable for a wide range of ages, offering a quiet and engaging activity.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hackney Downs
transport.tip: Check term times before visiting

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed space with books and stories suitable for a wide range of ages, offering a quiet and engaging activity.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hackney Downs
transport.tip: Check term times before visiting

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the history of planet Earth with dinosaur fossils and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Features a 25.2-metre-long blue whale skeleton and dinosaur fossils, making it engaging for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with hands-on exhibits and interactive displays.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers hands-on experiences and experiment demonstrations, making it a great place for kids to discover science.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Book tickets for Wonderlab for an extra hands-on experience.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Museum with stuffed animals, musical instruments, anthropology gallery, gardens, and farm animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of indoor exhibits and outdoor spaces, including gardens and animals, suitable for a 3-year-old.

transport.tip: Check out the views over London from the gardens.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm
activities.description: City farm with a variety of animals, Easter hunts, and bonnet-making crafts.
activities.whyItWorks: The free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts, combined with the opportunity to see animals, make this a fun and engaging activity for a 3-year-old.

transport.tip: Check the website for specific Easter event timings.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: London's highest public garden with 360° views of the city.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a spectacular view and a unique experience, and the greenery is a bonus.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bank, then a short walk
transport.tip: Book in advance as spaces are limited.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Museum with child-friendly tours, play spaces, and a giant map.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers child-friendly activities and play spaces, including the All Hands Children's Gallery and a role-play space for kids under 8.

transport.tip: Check for Character Encounters on Saturdays.

activities.title: Tower Bridge Family Activities
activities.description: Discover how the illusion of motion works at Tower Bridge with making, movement and mechanical marvels
activities.whyItWorks: Combines hands-on activities with the iconic landmark, giving children the chance to discover how Victorian engineers and inventors shaped the modern world.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Whitechapel, then take the District Line to Tower Hill station
transport.tip: Drop-in throughout the day.

activities.title: Laaaunch!
activities.description: Multi-sensory space-themed show.
activities.whyItWorks: A multi-sensory experience designed to stimulate and engage very young children.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: WordPlay: Ages 3 – 5
activities.description: Songs and storytelling for young children.
activities.whyItWorks: Engages young children with the joy of words and communication through sing-alongs and storytelling.

transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for specific dates and times.

activities.title: The Lion Inside
activities.description: A heartwarming tale about confidence and finding your roar.
activities.whyItWorks: Features engaging storytelling and a positive message, suitable for young children.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Imagine Children's Festival
activities.description: Festival of fun and creativity for families.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of shows, music, puppetry, and free activities, with events tailored for different age groups.

transport.tip: Study the programme beforehand and make bookings.

activities.title: Kew Gardens - Bluey at Kew
activities.description: Bluey-themed interactive activities including a play zone and character meet and greet.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive activities and the chance to meet a popular character make this very appealing to young children.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, check weather before you travel as mostly outdoor

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Easter egg hunt amongst the animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines the excitement of an egg hunt with the appeal of seeing animals.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Easter egg hunt in a historical environment.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Hobbledown Heath Easter Eggsplorers
activities.description: Hunt the Giant Easter Egg Trail, Meet the Easter Bunny, Indoor Playbarn, Outdoor Adventure Play Villages, Zoo and Farm Animals
activities.whyItWorks: Climb towers, crawl through tunnels, pan for crystals in the Mining Village, scramble through slides and levers, and bounce on giant Jumping and Water Pillows.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then take the Central Line to the Hounslow West station
transport.tip: Most families spend 4–5 hours, with many staying for the full day to explore everything the park has to offer.
